constraints.layout_style: nested sections with h3 headings instead of labels

required_complexity_patterns.notes: Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root
At least one readonly or disabled input with prefilled profile value
Inputs laid out in table cells, not flex card stacks only
Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form
Phone split into country code select and local number input in separate wrappers

Are you authorized to work in this country?

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, small, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root Required: At least one readonly or disabled input with prefilled profile value Required: Inputs laid out in table cells, not flex card stacks only Required: Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form Required: Phone split into country code select and local number input in separate wrappers Reference DOM (Placeholder-only identification): Empty label element; field identified only by placeholder attribute Reference DOM (SVG icon labels): Label contains SVG icon; accessible name via aria-labelledby only Reference DOM (Ashby yes no buttons): Ashby-inspired yes/no as two styled buttons instead of native radio Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: shadow-host, readonly-prefill, table-layout, decoy-form, split-phone. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: nested sections with h3 headings instead of labels. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Placeholder-only identification; SVG icon labels; Ashby yes no buttons.
